properties.remark: TWO 8FT SECTIONS OF A CEDAR FENCE WERE BLOWN OVER. TOTAL OF 16 FEET OF FENCE. POSTS WERE ROTTEN. WINDS ESTIMATED ABOUT 40 MPH AT THE TIME.
WATER RUNNING ANKLE DEEP TWO HOURS AGO IN ELEPHANT CANYON WHICH IS IN THE CENTRAL PORTION OF THE NEEDLES DISTRICT.
3 REPORTS OF A TORNADO THAT BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AND WENT BACK UP. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
PICTURE OF A TORNADO ON THE GROUND NEAR OR WEST OF MONTEZUMA CREEK SEEN ON TWITTER
BRIEF FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED ON SOME OF THE ROADS IN SOUTHEAST PORTIONS OF ARCHULETA COUNTY.
ROCKS AND MUD ACROSS THE HIGHWAY NEAR CLAY HALLS CROSSING DUE TO HEAVY RAIN AS WELL AS ACCUMULATION OF PENNY TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
PENNY TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL ACCUMULATED 4 TO 5 FEET DEEP
ONE FOOT OF DEBRIS FILLED WATER FLOWING OVER POTASH ROAD ON PARK BOUNDARY AT THE END OF PAVEMENT OF HWY 279.
NORMALLY DRY WASH RAGING AT BANKFULL FROM RAIN THIS AFTERNOON
ROAD WASHED OUT ON WEST SIDE OF KEBLER PASS
QUARTER SIZED HAIL REPORTED ON TOP OF WHITEROCKS MOUNTAIN. HAIL ACCUMULATED TO 3 INCHES DEEP IN SOME AREAS.
LARGE PINE AND ASPEN TREES SNAPPED NEAR BASE OF TREES AND SMALLER PINE TREES UPROOTED
APPROXIMATE 5 INCH DIAMETER LIMB DOWN ON LIVE TREE
MESA COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED 2 MINOR MUD SLIDES ALONG HIGHWAY 141... MILEPOST 109... ABOUT 4 TO 6 MILES NE OF GATEWAY
MESA COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED A MUD SLIDE ON EAST BOUND INTERSTATE 70 NEAR LOMA. EAST BOUND INTERSTATE 70 CLOSED FOR CLEAN UP.
MUDSLIDE REPORTED JUST NORTH OF GATEWAY AT MT POST 111.
MUD FLOWING ACROSS ROUTE 6 EAST OF PALISADE AT MILE MARKER 44 AND RAPID CREEK ROAD. NUMEROUS REPORTS FROM THE PUBLIC OF WATER AND MUD ACROSS ROADS AND ON PROPERTIES
FAST FLOWING WATER UP TO KNEE DEEP. BEGAN ABOUT 730 PM MDT AND WAS ONGOING.

WATER AND MUD ON AND ACROSS ROADS IN DOWNTOWN MOAB AND SOUTH OF TOWN AT INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 191 AND MILL CREEK ROAD. RAINFALL RUNOFF MORE THAN STREET GUTTERS COULD HANDLE WITH 2 TO 3 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS SOME INTERSECTIONS.
REPORT OF 4 SHINGLES THAT FELL OFF A ROOF.
PORTIONS OF TREES DOWN IN A YARD. ESTIMATED 40MPH GUST.
WIND GUST AT THE RIFLE AIRPORT
MUD OVER CR 5 BETWEEN MILEMARKERS 16 AND 21. ROADWAY IS PASSABLE.
POWER POLE WAS BLOWN DOWN IN SOUTH VERNAL BY STRONG THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS. POWER WAS OUT TO A PORTION OF THE CITY AS A RESULT.
1 INCH HAIL REPORTED CAUSING DAMAGE TO TREES.
WATER FLOWED ACROSS HIGHWAY 141
FLASH FLOODING IN THE DELICATE ARCH AREA... SALT WASH... SALT VALLEY WASH... AND WINTER CAMP WASH FROM 530PM WEDNESDAY. CAMPERS TRAPPED FROM 530PM UNTIL 845PM.
SALT WASH BEGAN RUNNING AT THE DELICATE ARCH ROAD AT 1130 PM SUNDAY. AT 1230 PM MONDAY FLOW IS 3.5 FEET DEEP AND 50 FEET WIDE. CAMPERS WERE TRAPPED UNTIL 1230 PM MONDAY.
WIND GUST MEASURED AT CANYONLANDS AIRPORT.
MALLARD DUCK KILLED BY LARGE HAIL.
ESTIMATED QUARTER-SIZED HAIL FALLING FOR APPROXIMATELY FIVE MINUTES. NO SIGNIFICANT RAINFALL TO REPORT.
LARGE HAIL OCCURRED FROM 325 TO 330 PM MDT.

RECEIVED 0.75 INCHES OF RAIN IN 25 MINUTES FROM 315 PM UNTIL 340 PM. NOTICED WATERFALLS FLOWING OVER MOON FLOWER CANYON WITH WATER FLOWING OVER CANE CREEK ROAD. CAMPERS HIKING OUT WITH SOAKED TENTS AND OTHER ITEMS. THE WATER HAS SINCE RECEEDED. DEBRIS NOTICEABLE OVER POT ASH ROAD AS EVIDENCE OF WATER PREVIOUSLY FLOWING OVER THAT ROAD.
WATER RAN 3 TO 4 FEET DEEP AND 40 TO 50 YARDS WIDE THROUGH WASHES AND OVER SOME ROADS AND TRAILS.
